dataQualityWarnings: The target StockX API response had no lowestAsk, sellFaster, or earnMore fields.
The only live buyer signal was a $600 highest bid against $3,598 retail.
No completed sale exists in the local capture for the Ghostface pendant.
Recent sterling Jacob & Co. Supreme comp data shows below-retail average sale behavior, so ask-side collector premiums should be distrusted.
The model uses analyst estimates for sale price because live ask-side market data was unavailable.

model.name: transparent comparable-weighted baseline
model.target: expected resale sale price before seller fees by horizon

colors.title: Supreme Jacob & Co. Ghostface Diamond Pendant Silver

method.detailedReasoning: The Ghostface Diamond Pendant cannot be scaffolded from StockX earnMore because the target market data returned no ask-side sale guidance. The only live buyer signal was a $600 bid versus $3,598 retail. Recent sterling Supreme Jacob & Co. pendant comps also warn against assuming premiums: the Fuck Em sterling pendant has recent average sale data below its own retail. The analyst estimate therefore treats any near-retail exit as speculative and models the pendant as a skip for resale despite collector appeal.
reviewQuestionsForCritic: Should the day-1 expected sale be even lower because the only live bid is $600?
Does the Ghostface shape and diamond setting justify a stronger collector premium than recent sterling Jacob & Co. comps?
Would private buyer demand for limited Jacob & Co. jewelry exist outside StockX enough to make this a maybe?
Should any high-ticket jewelry item be excluded from normal cop calls until completed sales exist?
Does the $3,598 retail price create too much fee drag for any marketplace resale path?

Silver.basis: only modeled color; value is driven by Jacob & Co. provenance, Ghostface collectibility, and diamond detail, but live bid support is far below retail.

horizonLogic.oneWeek: Without ask-side market formation, one-week value should fade below the speculative day-1 near-retail anchor.
horizonLogic.oneMonth: One-month value fades toward the visible buyer signal and recent below-retail sterling Jacob & Co. comp behavior.
horizonLogic.oneYear: One-year value can partially recover for a patient collector sale, but remains below retail unless completed sales prove real demand.

liveMarketInterpretation.interpretation: The StockX API shows a product shell and a $600 bid, but no lowestAsk, sellFaster, or earnMore. This is not a liquid market and cannot support a positive resale call.

confidenceAdjustment.basis: Confidence is low because the forecast is analyst-derived from missing ask-side data, a weak live bid, and indirect Jacob & Co. comps.

day1Predictions.liquidityNote: Only live StockX buyer signal was a $600 bid, with no lowest ask, sellFaster, or earnMore. Treat any near-retail exit as speculative private-buyer upside.
day1Predictions.analystOverrideReason: No StockX earnMore was available; estimate is a conservative below-retail placeholder based on weak bid support and below-retail sterling Jacob & Co. comp behavior.

horizonPredictions.liquidityNote: High-ticket collector item with no live ask-side market; patient private sale may outperform, but marketplace liquidity is too thin for resale.

colorLevelSummaries.reasoning: Silver is the only colorway. The pendant is collectible on concept, but the live StockX board is not supportive: no ask-side sale guidance and only a $600 bid against $3,598 retail. Even a speculative $2,800 resale would lose more than $1,100 after fees and handling, so this is a skip for resale.

overall.baseRecommendation: Skip for resale; collector-only personal buy if the user wants the piece regardless of market liquidity.
keyRisks: No StockX lowest ask, sellFaster, or earnMore values were available for the target pendant.
The only live bid was $600 against $3,598 retail.
Marketplace fees create severe drag: even a near-retail sale loses hundreds after fees and handling.
Recent sterling Supreme Jacob & Co. pendant comp behavior includes below-retail average sales.
High-ticket jewelry has a very thin buyer pool and may require patient private sale channels rather than StockX.
Retail price source confidence remains medium because the app originally sourced price from DropsGG/Reddit and secondary previews, not an official Supreme product page capture.
Completed sales were not collected for this first pass.
